purpose: Defines the !scope {task} slash command — blast-radius analysis BEFORE touching any code. Determines which directories, files, and contracts a proposed change would affect; helps user decide whether to proceed, simplify, or split into phases. Three-step workflow: parse task (new feature / refactor / bug fix / migration; which domains; additive vs modificative), search for affected files (grep + glob + .synapse/toc.md + import/export tracing for indirect deps + cross-layer contract consumers + config/env/deploy scripts), produce analysis (Impact Summary / Affected Files by Area / Cross-Layer Impact / Dependencies & Ordering / Risks / Recommendation: Proceed-as-is | Break-into-phases | Simplify-first | Needs-more-investigation). Optional dep_graph.json consultation: if {project_root}/.synapse/dep_graph.json exists, look up importers + imports + transitive importers to capture full blast radius beyond manual import tracing.
gotchas: Be HONEST about complexity — don't minimize scope to make the task seem easier; user needs accurate info.
INCLUDE indirect effects: a type rename might touch 50 files; a new endpoint might require auth+validation+tests+frontend integration. Surface all of it.
dep_graph.json is OPTIONAL but valuable: provides transitive importer chains that manual tracing misses. Falls back gracefully when absent.
Recommendation must be one of four explicit options — not vague hedging.
patterns: Pre-coding analysis: parse intent → find affected files → trace cross-layer → recommend.
Optional dep_graph.json enrichment for transitive deps.
Four-option recommendation: proceed / break / simplify / investigate.
conventions: Output sections: Impact Summary / Affected Files / Cross-Layer / Dependencies / Risks / Recommendation.
